commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed for the missing real behavior proof and the compatibility/security-boundary decision; I did not find a narrow code repair for automation.; Cleared: No supply-chain, dependency, secret-handling, or permission broadening concern was found; the diff strengthens an iMessage authorization boundary, with compatibility risk tracked separately. (sha=7d66fca5c193b92fc23af0260e84365950fc9c4a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an iMessage outbound allowlist assertion and threads trusted reply-sender context through channel delivery so sends and private-API message/group actions fail before transport calls when policy disallows the target.

PR surface: Source +426, Tests +1329. Total +1755 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reaches iMessage send/action transport paths without the new outbound allowlist gate, and the PR adds focused blocked-target regressions. I did not run a live iMessage account reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for the missing real behavior proof and the compatibility/security-boundary decision; I did not find a narrow code repair for automation.

target.title: fix: enforce iMessage outbound allowlist
target.body:
## Summary

- Enforce iMessage outbound allowlist checks before private API probing or send calls for reply, sendWithEffect, upload-file, and normal send paths.
- Keep direct-message and group-target policies separate while preserving configured accessGroup expansion.
- Add blocked-target and allowlisted-target coverage for the affected send paths.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/actions.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: iMessage outbound sends now fail closed before transport/runtime calls when the configured allowlist does not include the handle or group target.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at PR head `cb227048c2`; commands ran against the actual OpenClaw iMessage extension code, with no live iMessage account or private API secrets used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/actions.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts`; `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/actions.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  71 passed (71)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.21s

$ pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled
$ node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs
exit status: 0

$ git diff --check
exit status: 0
```

Observed result after fix: Blocked-target paths reject before `runtimeMock.sendRichMessage`, `runtimeMock.sendAttachment`, or `client.request` are called; allowlisted paths still call `client.request` with the expected iMessage payload. The bundled-extension lint command exits 0.

What was not tested: A live iMessage private API send was not run from this machine; this PR intentionally avoids live account credentials and validates the fail-closed behavior in the extension runtime.

## Fork Context

This is a temporary upstream-review branch. The same accepted fix remains on `LDMB123/openclaw` fork `main`, per the fork cleanup plan.

target.title: fix: resolve QMD Windows shims and guard image URL downloads
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the Windows QMD shim path and narrows the image URL work into a guarded provider-download path.

- Resolve Windows `.cmd` shims that point at quoted absolute JavaScript entrypoints.
- Use the real QMD package name, `@tobilu/qmd`, for package-bin fallback resolution.
- Parse OpenAI-compatible image responses that return `data[].url` instead of `data[].b64_json`.
- Route provider-returned image URLs through guarded HTTP with bounded reads, MIME validation, scoped same-origin transport reuse, configured media limits, and operation-deadline timeouts.
- Dropped the unrelated `node-llama-cpp` dependency/lockfile churn from the original PR scope.

## Behavior addressed

Windows QMD availability checks no longer fail when `qmd.cmd` wraps a quoted absolute Node entrypoint instead of a `%~dp0`-relative entrypoint. OpenAI-compatible URL-backed image generation responses now produce image assets without using raw fetch or broad private-network carryover.

## Real environment tested

- AWS Crabbox native Windows: `cbx_36917d25d801`, run `run_0dd48bf72083`, hydrate workflow `26722175678`.
- AWS Crabbox Windows WSL2: `cbx_92cdb109d982`, run `run_740f8e92e641`, hydrate workflow `26721781232`.
- AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate after rebase: `cbx_0b074a7498e9`, run `run_72f46782c53c`.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts src/image-generation/image-assets.test.ts src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.test.ts
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
node scripts/docs-list.js
node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs
git diff --check
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Native Windows and WSL2 ran the same focused Vitest set on hydrated Crabbox workspaces. The final Linux changed gate was run after rebasing onto current `origin/main`.

## Evidence after fix

- Local post-rebase focused tests: 6 shards passed, 218 tests total.
- Native Windows Crabbox focused tests: 6 shards passed, 213 tests total on `run_0dd48bf72083`.
- WSL2 Crabbox focused tests: 6 shards passed, 215 tests total on `run_740f8e92e641`.
- Plugin SDK API baseline check passed after regenerating the baseline across the final rebase.
- SDK subpath export check passed.
- Autoreview passed clean after the URL-download security/timeout/boundary fixes.

## Observed result after fix

- Quoted absolute Windows `.cmd` QMD shims resolve to direct Node execution without shell fallback.
- Scoped QMD package fallback resolves through `@tobilu/qmd`.
- URL-backed OpenAI-compatible image responses are accepted and converted into generated image assets.
- Cross-origin provider-returned URLs do not inherit provider private-network/dispatcher/auth policy.
- Same-origin provider-returned URLs keep provider auth/transport policy and still use guarded fetch.
- URL downloads honor configured generated-media byte limits and share the image operation deadline instead of resetting timeout per download.

## What was not tested

Full live QMD memory indexing/search was not run. The final `pnpm check:changed` gate is blocked by a current `origin/main` type error in untouched `src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts` from `b0679d1f13d`; this PR does not touch channel ingress queue files.
target.author: knightplat-blip

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts src/image-generation/image-assets.test.ts src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.test.ts
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
node scripts/docs-list.js
node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs
git diff --check
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Native Windows and WSL2 ran the same focused Vitest set on hydrated Crabbox workspaces. The final Linux changed gate was run after rebasing onto current `origin/main`.

## Evidence after fix

- Local post-rebase focused tests: 6 shards passed, 218 tests total.
- Native Windows Crabbox focused tests: 6 shards passed, 213 tests total on `run_0dd48bf72083`.
- WSL2 Crabbox focused tests: 6 shards passed, 215 tests total on `run_740f8e92e641`.
- Plugin SDK API baseline check passed after regenerating the baseline across the final rebase.
- SDK subpath export check passed.
- Autoreview passed clean after the URL-download security/timeout/boundary fixes.

## Observed result after fix

- Quoted absolute Windows `.cmd` QMD shims resolve to direct Node execution without shell fallback.
- Scoped QMD package fallback resolves through `@tobilu/qmd`.
- URL-backed OpenAI-compatible image responses are accepted and converted into generated image assets.
- Cross-origin provider-returned URLs do not inherit provider private-network/dispatcher/auth policy.
- Same-origin provider-returned URLs keep provider auth/transport policy and still use guarded fetch.
- URL downloads honor configured generated-media byte limits and share the image operation deadline instead of resetting timeout per download.

## What was not tested

Full live QMD memory indexing/search was not run. The final `pnpm check:changed` gate is blocked by a current `origin/main` type error in untouched `src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts` from `b0679d1f13d`; this PR does not touch channel ingress queue files.
